title: Shoals Technologies Stock Falls 11% on Earnings Miss Despite Revenue Beat
text: Shoals Technologies Group (NASDAQ: SHLS) dropped 11.21% in pre-market trading following its fourth-quarter earnings report on Tuesday. The company delivered a revenue beat but disappointed on profitability. Revenue surged 39% year-over-year to $148.3 million, exceeding analyst expectations of $145.5 million. However, adjusted earnings per share came in at $0.10, missing the consensus estimate of $0.13. Gross profit increased to $46.9 million from $40.2 million previously, yet gross margin compressed to 31.6% from 37.6%. The decline resulted from increased material costs, $3.3 million in duties and tariffs, and $1.5 million in right-of-use asset amortization. CEO Brandon Moss expressed satisfaction with performance, noting the company achieved record backlog and awarded orders of $747.6 million, an 18% increase from year-end 2024. The growth stems from heightened demand for utility-scale solar projects. For Q1 2026, management expects $125-135 million in revenue (midpoint $130 million), surpassing analyst consensus of $118.8 million. Full-year 2026 revenue guidance stands at $560-600 million.
